generated_on: RTX 4070 Laptop (sm_89), 8 GB VRAM, 32 GB RAM, Windows 11

quantized_mode.validation_notes: Quantization intrinsically introduces numerical drift vs safetensors/F64. Q8_0 is an 8-bit quantization format with better precision than Q4_K_M. Validation: greedy generation smoke test passed successfully. Full forward vs safetensors: max_abs_diff 2.28 (4.6x ADR-004 threshold). Argmax match: 4/4 (better than Q4_K_M due to higher precision). F16 tensors (norm): drift 0.0. Q8_0 tensors decode with moderate drift. This is expected behavior for Q8_0 quantization.

recommended_mode_rationale: GGUF models use quantization formats (Q4_K_M, Q8_0) that intrinsically introduce numerical drift beyond ADR-004 strict thresholds. This is not an Atenia bug but a property of the quantization itself. Validation is functional: smoke tests confirm model loads and generates coherent text. Q8_0 drift 2.28 vs safetensors is within expected range for 8-bit quantization and has better argmax consistency (4/4) than Q4_K_M (1/4). Use quantized mode for all GGUF workloads; certified/fast mode distinction does not apply to quantized formats.
per_tensor_policy_status: GGUF models are fully quantized (Q8_0). Per-tensor mode switching (certified/fast) is not applicable; all tensors use the quantized format. F16 tensors (norm, some embeddings) decode to F32 without re-quantization. Q8_0 tensors decode to F32 via Q8_0 decoder. Mode selection is per-model format, not per-tensor.

drift_diagnostics.full_forward_test: Compare Q8_0 GGUF forward vs safetensors baseline

gguf_specific.intrinsic_drift_note: Q8_0 quantization introduces 8-bit precision loss. Drift vs safetensors is expected and intrinsic to the format. The model remains functionally correct for generation tasks despite numerical drift. Q8_0 has better precision than Q4_K_M (argmax 4/4 vs 1/4).
known_limitations: Numerical drift vs safetensors exceeds ADR-004 threshold by 4.6x
Not suitable for workloads requiring exact numerical equivalence to F64
Best for chat/completion with greedy or low-temperature sampling
Higher precision than Q4_K_M but still quantized

comparison_with_q4_k_m.recommendation: Use Q8_0 when precision matters more than size; use Q4_K_M for maximum compression
